In 1800, clear glass windows were a luxury. In Europe, they were heavily taxed (the British Window Tax wasn't repealed until 1851). You cannot look *through* these materials; you have to open them to look *out*. In 1800, roughly 90% of the world's population lived in rural, agricultural settings. The vast majority of people (including young children) worked from dawn until dusk outdoors in fields, on farms, or in manual labor. They simply weren't indoors during the day enough to idly gaze out of windows.
